title: erigeron altaicus
intro: Erigeron altaicus, also known as Dandelion's Cousin or Alpine Fleabane, is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of fever and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Erigeron altaicus include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of certain cancers, and support the immune system. Erigeron altaicus can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
